hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5/ECL2 interactions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity in the SSTR2-unique ECL3 pocket, thereby increasing selectivity margin over off-target subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but lack consistent selectivity. Future iterations should prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to disrupt off-target interactions and increase Δmargin across the candidate set.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, focus mutations should target non-pharmacophore positions that are known to contribute to off-target interactions (particularly with SSTR3/SSTR5).
Introduce mutations at key non-pharmacophore positions to reduce off-target binding. These mutations are chosen to disrupt conserved interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting SSTR2 binding.
